user_query:
Please review the proposed changes to our payments API by diffing the two OpenAPI specs in input/baseline.yaml and input/proposed.yaml. Deliver the following:

1) Generate a machine-readable JSON diff highlighting breaking and non-breaking changes and save it to output/diff.json.

2) From the diff, create a CSV that lists ONLY breaking changes with the exact header:
rule,entity,location,before,after,details
Save it to output/breaking_changes.csv. Use these conventions:
- rule: one of endpoint-removed, required-param-added, field-type-changed, response-field-removed, enum-value-removed, auth-requirement-changed
- entity: endpoint | parameter | schema | security (choose the most appropriate)
- location:
  - For endpoints: "GET /path", "POST /path", etc.
  - For schema properties: a JSON pointer like "#/components/schemas/Transaction/properties/amount"
  - For security changes: a concise label such as "global security"
- before/after: brief type/value summary (e.g., number → string, [USD, EUR] → [USD], Bearer → API Key)
- details: short free-text context

Make sure the CSV includes at least the following breaking changes, if present in the diff:
- Removal of GET /v1/transactions
- New required query parameter includeBalances on GET /v1/accounts/{id}
- Type change on Transaction.amount from number to string
- Removal of Transaction.metadata from responses
- Removal of enum value EUR from Transaction.currency
- Change to global auth requirement (from bearer token to API key)

3) Write a detailed migration plan to output/migration_plan.md with the following sections: Breaking Changes (enumerate each), Mitigations, Versioning/Deprecation Strategy, Communication Plan, Testing/Validation, and Timeline. Address each of the breaking changes listed above with concrete mitigations (e.g., compatibility layers, deprecation windows, version bumps, fallback behavior). Also include an appendix summarizing any lint findings on input/proposed.yaml.

Notes:
- Read only from input/.
- Write all artifacts under output/ using the exact paths above.
- Ensure the CSV header matches exactly and that all required breaking changes are represented.
